Things I Built at Work

Shipping AI at Scale

PM at Sprinklr, contributing to a $200M ARR platform. Here's what I've been building.

Sprinklr · AI Assistant

Social Copilot

An AI-driven assistant that streamlines social media workflows — post discovery, insights, approvals, and scheduling — via natural language. Improved team efficiency by 40% and validated across 20 enterprise beta clients before rollout.

The Problem

Enterprise social teams juggle dozens of manual steps — drafting, scheduling, approvals, performance lookups — across disconnected tools. The overhead scales badly with team size, and nothing was designed to let a manager get answers in plain English.

What I Built
  • Owned end-to-end product development of Sprinklr Copilot from PRD to GA, covering natural language task execution across the entire social workflow stack.
  • Conducted 30+ user interviews and adoption analysis to identify workflow friction and translate it into a prioritised feature roadmap.
  • Collaborated with QA and engineering to validate Copilot functionality across diverse use cases before beta launch.
  • Created field collateral for GTM beta with 20 clients, gathering structured feedback to drive UX iterations.

Outcome: 40% improvement in workflow efficiency. Copilot now powers workflows for 1,500+ enterprise clients, with Microsoft and Cisco among early adopters.

Sprinklr · Partnerships & Integrations

Platform Integrations & Partnerships

Managed strategic API integrations and partnerships with Meta, Snapchat, and Reddit — aligning partner requirements with Sprinklr's product roadmap to maximise ecosystem value for enterprise clients.

The Problem

Social platform APIs evolve constantly. Keeping a multi-channel enterprise platform in sync requires not just technical alignment, but product-level negotiation with platform partners — balancing what partners want to expose with what enterprise clients actually need.

What I Built
  • Spearheaded 20+ feature developments across social channels for clients including Microsoft, Deutsche Telekom, and Cisco — driving $800K ARR in contract renewals.
  • Managed strategic partnerships with Meta, Snapchat, and Reddit, translating partner API capabilities into client-ready product features.
  • Increased platform usability by 30% through reducing redundant touchpoints, improving UX flows, and introducing contextual in-app guidance.
  • Contributed to scaling the platform to 1,500+ clients and $200M ARR through targeted product initiatives.

Outcome: $800K in contract renewals directly attributable to feature launches. Platform usability improved by 30% across high-volume client workflows.

Sprinklr · ML-Powered Moderation

Spam Moderation Engine

Built a configurable, multi-tenant ML classification system that separates genuine customer interactions from noise — designed around deliberate trade-offs rather than raw accuracy optimisation.

The Problem

Enterprise brands receive tens of thousands of social media messages every day. Community managers cannot read every one. Genuine customer complaints get missed. Agents burn out clearing spam. The deeper challenge wasn't just building a classifier — it was that no single threshold works for every brand, no feedback signal is perfectly clean, and retraining a live model in a multi-tenant environment carries real risk.

Key Trade-offs I Made
  • Recall over precision, made configurable: Noise outweighs signal 3:1 on social data. I defaulted to recall — catching every genuine interaction — while surfacing threshold configuration as an explicit, brand-level parameter for high-volume clients who needed tighter queues.
  • Brand-specific corrections, not global retraining: Agent feedback adjusts a brand's fine-tuning layer only — keeping the global base model stable across all clients.
  • Volume-gated retraining: Set a minimum correction volume per error category before retraining triggers, trading short-term accuracy for long-term stability.
  • F1 (F-beta) as the deployment gate: Prevents optimising purely for recall (flags everything) or precision (flags nothing). F-beta > 1 for sensitive verticals.
  • Rule layer for brand-specific edge cases: Competitor mentions, profanity thresholds, and regional filters handled at the rule engine — not baked into model weights — keeping the classifier auditable and cheap to update.

Outcome: 80%+ classification accuracy, matching human team performance. Reduced False Negative rate in high-priority categories without proportional agent queue increases. Client success teams got a defensible, data-backed story for every threshold decision.

Personal Projects

Building on Weekends

Things I've shipped outside of work — exploring what's possible when you combine product thinking with vibe coding.

Live

OpenDiagnosys

An AI-driven clinical decision-support platform for doctors. Built to close the gap between a clinician's memory and the full breadth of current medical literature — delivering differential insights, evidence-backed summaries, and multi-source document analysis in seconds.

Integrated LLM APIs, domain-specific medical LLMs, embedding-based semantic search, and RAG pipelines — reducing manual research time for doctors by 70%. Designed the entire product experience and iterated on information architecture through direct clinician feedback.

Visit opendiagnosys.com →
In Progress

Social Media Automation

A micro-service platform for intelligent social media management built for D2C brands and creators. The problem: most scheduling tools are dumb — they post, they don't think. This system adds a layer of AI-driven content strategy, audience timing intelligence, and cross-platform orchestration.

Combining what I've learned building enterprise social tools at Sprinklr with LLM-powered automation — designed specifically for teams that move fast and don't have a community manager on staff.
